3	  id: plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout
4	  depends_on:

7	    - FEAT-018
8	---
9	# DDx Skill Package Layout
10	
11	Date: 2026-05-13
12	Status: Draft
13	
14	## Summary
15	
16	The DDx skill tree should be content owned by the default `ddx` plugin package,
17	not a parallel bootstrap tree with checked-in mirrors. `ddx init`, `ddx update`,
18	and `ddx plugin install ddx` should all install the same package layout through
19	the same package installer.
20	
21	Development installs may use symlink overlays for live editing, but those
22	overlays are project-local. DDx must not restore home-directory skill installs;
23	the only expected home binary is `~/.local/bin/ddx`.
24	
25	## Current Problems
26	
27	- `skills/ddx/` is the editable source, but `cli/internal/skills/ddx/`,
28	  `.agents/skills/ddx/`, `.claude/skills/ddx/`, and `.ddx/skills/ddx/` are
29	  full copied mirrors.
30	- `cli/Makefile copy-skills` syncs the top-level skill tree into embedded and
31	  project-local copies, so editing behavior depends on remembering which mirror
32	  is canonical.
33	- `ddx init` installs shipped skills through a direct bootstrap path using
34	  `skills.SkillFiles`, then separately installs the default `ddx` plugin
35	  package.
36	- `ddx update` refreshes shipped skills through the same direct embedded-skill
37	  path rather than the package installer.
38	- `ddx plugin install ddx --local .` is unsafe in the current repo layout
39	  because local install prefers `<pluginRoot>/.agents/skills` before
40	  `<pluginRoot>/skills`; pointed at the repo root, that can target the
41	  destination skill tree instead of the intended source.
42	
43	## Target Layout
44	
45	The default package owns the DDx skill:
46	
47	```text
48	library/
49	  package.yaml
50	  skills/
51	    ddx/
52	      SKILL.md
53	      reference/
54	      bead-lifecycle/
55	      ...
56	```
57	
58	The checked-in editable source is `library/skills/ddx/`.
59	
60	The release binary embeds the default package content, not a special skill-only
61	tree:
62	
63	```text
64	library/ -> cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/
65	```
66	
67	The project-local discovered skill paths are install outputs:
68	
69	```text
70	.agents/skills/ddx  -> installed by package installer
71	.claude/skills/ddx  -> installed by package installer
72	```
73	
74	For registry installs, these outputs are real files. For local development
75	overlays, they may be symlinks to the source checkout.
76	
77	## Manifest Contract
78	
79	`library/package.yaml` declares package-local skill sources:
80	
81	```yaml
82	install:
83	  root:
84	    source: .
85	    target: .ddx/plugins/ddx
86	  skills:
87	    - source: skills/
88	      target: .agents/skills/
89	    - source: skills/
90	      target: .claude/skills/
91	```
92	
93	The installer must honor `install.skills[*].source` relative to the package
94	root. Skill discovery must not hard-code `.agents/skills` as the primary source
95	when the manifest explicitly says `skills/`.
96	
97	Directory-to-directory skill mappings install the child skill directories under
98	the target. The example above installs `skills/ddx` to `.agents/skills/ddx` and
99	`.claude/skills/ddx`; it does not replace `.agents/skills` or `.claude/skills`
100	as whole directories. This keeps unrelated project skills visible.
101	
102	## Installer Contract
103	
104	Split the package installer into source-agnostic operations:
105	
106	```go
107	InstallPackageFromRemote(pkg, projectRoot)
108	InstallPackageFromDir(pkg, sourceDir, projectRoot)
109	InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, sourceFS, projectRoot)
110	```
111	
112	All three paths must share the same core install implementation:
113	
114	1. load and validate `package.yaml` when present;
115	2. apply `install.root.source -> install.root.target`;
116	3. apply every `install.skills[*].source -> install.skills[*].target`;
117	4. apply scripts/executables with the existing project-scope rules;
118	5. return one `InstalledEntry` shape.
119	
120	Remote and embedded installs write real files. Local installs create developer
121	overlays: `.ddx/plugins/<name>` and plugin-owned skill outputs are symlinks to
122	the local checkout. Local overlays do not mutate recorded plugin pins and do not
123	auto-commit.
124	
125	## Init And Update Contract
126	
127	`ddx init` installs the default DDx package through the embedded package
128	installer. It no longer calls a separate embedded skill installer and no longer
129	maintains `.ddx/skills/ddx` as a bootstrap-only mirror.
130	
131	`ddx update` / the forward replacement for shipped-content refresh uses the
132	same package installer path as `ddx init`.
133	
134	Remove these direct bootstrap mechanisms after replacement tests exist:
135	
136	- `skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, ...)` calls from `ddx init` and update;
137	- `registerBootstrapDDxSkills`;
138	- `cli/internal/skills/ddx` as a distinct embedded source;
139	- `skills/ddx` as the top-level canonical source;
140	- checked-in `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx`, and `.ddx/skills/ddx`
141	  mirrors.
142	
143	## Development Workflow
144	
145	The repo development workflow becomes:
146	
147	```bash
148	ddx plugin install ddx --local library --force
149	```
150	
151	After that command, edits to `library/skills/ddx/` are live through the
152	project-local harness discovery paths.
153	
154	The command must be safe when run from the DDx repo. If someone passes the repo
155	root instead of `library`, the installer must either resolve to the manifest's
156	declared package root or fail with a clear error; it must not self-link
157	`.agents/skills/ddx`.
158	
159	## Migration Sequence
160	
161	1. Add embedded default-plugin package support for `library/`.
162	2. Teach the installer to honor `install.skills[*].source` for remote,
163	   embedded, and local installs.
164	3. Change `ddx init` to install `ddx` through the embedded package installer.
165	4. Change shipped-content refresh/update to use the same package installer.
166	5. Move `skills/ddx/` to `library/skills/ddx/`.
167	6. Replace `copy-skills` with a default-package embed sync:
168	   `library/ -> cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/`.
169	7. Remove the checked-in skill mirrors after tests prove install outputs are
170	   produced by init/local install.
171	8. Update FEAT-011, FEAT-015, SD-011, and any command help or AGENTS blocks that
172	   describe copied bootstrap skill mirrors.
173	
174	## Required Tests
175	
176	- `TestInitInstallsDDxPluginPackage`: `ddx init` creates `.ddx/plugins/ddx`,
177	  `.agents/skills/ddx`, and `.claude/skills/ddx` through the package installer.
178	- `TestPluginInstallLocalDDxLibrarySymlinksSkills`: `ddx plugin install ddx
179	  --local library --force` creates project-local symlinks to
180	  `library/skills/ddx`.
181	- `TestPluginInstallHonorsSkillSource`: a fixture with `install.skills.source:
182	  skills/` installs skills from `skills/` and does not require
183	  `.agents/skills` inside the source package.
184	- `TestPluginInstallPreservesUnrelatedProjectSkills`: installing the `ddx`
185	  package does not replace `.agents/skills` or `.claude/skills` when other
186	  skills already exist there.
187	- `TestInitWorksOfflineWithEmbeddedDefaultPlugin`: no network is needed for
188	  `ddx init` to install the default package and skill.
189	- `TestRepoRootLocalInstallDoesNotSelfLink`: passing the DDx repo root either
190	  resolves to the package root safely or fails before modifying skill outputs.
191	- `ddx skills check library/skills/ddx .agents/skills/ddx .claude/skills/ddx`
192	  passes after local install.
193	- `make skill-schema` validates the package-owned skill source and embedded
194	  default-package copy.
195	
196	## Non-Scope
197	
198	- Do not restore home-directory skill installs.
199	- Do not change the public plugin CLI names beyond the separate plugin CLI
200	  rationalization plan.
201	- Do not remove the embedded package copy until offline `ddx init` is green.
202

17	// UpdateOptions represents update command configuration
18	type UpdateOptions struct {
19		Check       bool
20		Force       bool
21		Reset       bool
22		Sync        bool
23		Strategy    string
24		Backup      bool
25		Interactive bool
26		Abort       bool

29	}

32	type ConflictInfo struct {
33		FilePath     string
34		LineNumber   int
35		ConflictType string
36		LocalContent string
37		TheirContent string
38		BaseContent  string
39	}
40	
41	// UpdateResult represents the result of an update operation
42	type UpdateResult struct {
43		Success      bool
44		Message      string
45		UpdatedFiles []string
46		Conflicts    []ConflictInfo
47		BackupPath   string
48	}
49	
50	// CommandFactory method - CLI interface layer
51	func (f *CommandFactory) runUpdate(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
52		// Extract flags to options struct
53		opts, err := extractUpdateOptions(cmd, args)
54		if err != nil {
55			return err
56		}
57	
58		// Call pure business logic
59		result, err := performUpdate(f.WorkingDir, opts)
60		if err != nil {
61			return err
62		}
63	
64		// Handle output formatting
65		return displayUpdateResult(cmd, result, opts)
66	}
67	
68	// performUpdate checks GitHub for the latest version of each installed plugin
69	// and updates any that are outdated (or all if --force). Always refreshes the
70	// embedded `ddx` skill and the AGENTS.md block so projects that ran `ddx init`
71	// under an older DDx version pick up current skill content without re-running
72	// init. Binary updates are intentionally explicit via `ddx upgrade`; `ddx
73	// update` must not replace a locally-built dogfood binary with the latest
74	// public release.
75	func performUpdate(workingDir string, opts *UpdateOptions) (*UpdateResult, error) {
76		// Refresh the shipped `ddx` skill copy + AGENTS.md block first, regardless
77		// of whether any plugins are installed. This is what lets older projects
78		// pick up new SKILL.md / reference/*.md content without re-init.
79		refreshShippedSkills(workingDir)
80	
81		state, err := registry.LoadState()
82		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
83			return &UpdateResult{Success: true, Message: "Shipped skills refreshed. No packages installed."}, nil
84		}
85	
86		reg := registry.BuiltinRegistry()
87	
88		var updated []string
89	
90		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
91			// Filter to specific target if requested.
92			if opts.Resource != "" && entry.Name != opts.Resource {
93				continue
94			}
95	
96			pkg, err := reg.Find(entry.Name)
97			if err != nil {
98				continue // not in registry, skip
99			}
100	
101			// Fetch actual latest version from GitHub.
102			latestVersion := pkg.Version
103			if release, err := update.FetchLatestReleaseForRepo(pkg.Source); err == nil {
104				latestVersion = strings.TrimPrefix(release.TagName, "v")
105			}
106	
107			if !opts.Force && entry.Version == latestVersion {
108				continue
109			}
110	
111			// Install the latest version.
112			installPkg := *pkg
113			installPkg.Version = latestVersion
114			newEntry, err := registry.InstallPackage(&installPkg, workingDir)
115			if err != nil {
116				return nil, fmt.Errorf("updating %s: %w", entry.Name, err)
117			}
118			state.AddOrUpdate(newEntry)
119			updated = append(updated, entry.Name+" "+entry.Version+" → "+latestVersion)
120		}
